meta.title: Drawer
meta.description: A foundational drawer component for overlays that require user attention and interaction. Built with React Aria Components for accessibility and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.

mdx:
## Overview

Drawers are overlay windows that appear on top of the main content to display
important information, gather user input, or require user decisions. They
temporarily disable the main interface until the user completes an action or
dismisses the drawer.

### Key features

- **Accessibility First**: Built with React Aria Components for WCAG 2.1 AA
  compliance
- **Flexible Positioning**: Support for left, right, top, and bottom placements
- **Responsive Sizing**: Multiple size variants from xs to full-screen
- **Smooth Animations**: Customizable motion presets for entrance/exit
- **Focus Management**: Automatic focus trapping and restoration
- **Keyboard Navigation**: Full keyboard support including Escape to close
- **Scroll Handling**: Options for inside or outside scrolling behavior

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[React Aria Drawer Docs](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Drawer.html)
[ARIA Drawer Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/)
[Figma Design Library](https://www.figma.com/design/gHbAJGfcrCv7f2bgzUQgHq/NIMBUS-Guidelines?node-id=1695-45519&m)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Basic Usage

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Drawer.Root>
    <Drawer.Trigger>
      Anything can be a trigger
    </Drawer.Trigger>
    <Drawer.Content>
      <Drawer.Header>
        <Drawer.Title>Drawer Title</Drawer.Title>
        <Drawer.CloseTrigger />
      </Drawer.Header>
      <Drawer.Body>
          This is a basic drawer with default settings. It includes a backdrop overlay
          (enabled by default), title, and close button for a complete experience.
      </Drawer.Body>
      <Drawer.Footer>
        <Button variant="outline" slot="close">Cancel</Button>
        <Button>Save</Button>
      </Drawer.Footer>
    </Drawer.Content>
  </Drawer.Root>
)
```

### Size

Control the drawer width by applying `width` prop on the Content component, it's
a regular style-prop, so you can use all size-tokens but also custom values.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Drawer.Root >
    <Drawer.Trigger asChild>
      <Button>Open Large Drawer</Button>
    </Drawer.Trigger>
    <Drawer.Content width="3xl">
      <Drawer.Header>
        <Drawer.Title>Large Drawer</Drawer.Title>
        <Drawer.CloseTrigger />
      </Drawer.Header>
      <Drawer.Body>
        This drawer uses a custom width. You can change the width by
        adjusting the width prop on Drawer.Root to values like "xs", "sm",
        "md", "lg", "xl", or "full", but also raw css values.
      </Drawer.Body>
    </Drawer.Content>
  </Drawer.Root>
)
```

### Placement Options

Position the drawer at different locations using the `placement` prop. You can
also control the backdrop overlay with the `showBackdrop` prop (enabled by
default):

```jsx live
const App = () => {
  const [showBackdrop, setShowBackdrop] = useState(true);

  const placements = [
    { value: "left", label: "Left", description: "Slides in from the left side, ideal for navigation panels or filters" },
    { value: "right", label: "Right (default)", description: "Slides in from the right side, commonly used for details panels and forms" },
    { value: "top", label: "Top", description: "Slides in from the top, useful for notifications or alerts" },
    { value: "bottom", label: "Bottom", description: "Slides in from the bottom, good for mobile-friendly sheets or action panels" }
  ];

  return (
    <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
      <Checkbox isSelected={showBackdrop} onChange={setShowBackdrop}>Show backdrop</Checkbox>

      <Stack direction="row" gap="400" wrap="wrap">
        {placements.map((placement) => (
          <Drawer.Root key={placement.value} placement={placement.value} showBackdrop={showBackdrop}>
            <Drawer.Trigger asChild>
              <Button>{placement.label}</Button>
            </Drawer.Trigger>
            <Drawer.Content>
              <Drawer.Header>
                <Drawer.Title>{placement.label} Placement</Drawer.Title>
                <Drawer.CloseTrigger />
              </Drawer.Header>
              <Drawer.Body>
                {placement.description}
              </Drawer.Body>
            </Drawer.Content>
          </Drawer.Root>
        ))}
      </Stack>
    </Stack>
  );
}
```

### Controlled State

Use external state to control the dialog programmatically:

```jsx live
const App = () => {
  const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = React.useState(false);

  return (
    <Stack>
      <Button alignSelf="flex-start" onPress={() => setIsOpen(true)}>
        Open Controlled Drawer
      </Button>

      <Text>Drawer is currently: {isOpen ? "open" : "closed"}</Text>

      <Drawer.Root isOpen={isOpen} onOpenChange={setIsOpen}>
        <Drawer.Content>
          <Drawer.Header>
            <Drawer.Title>Controlled Drawer</Drawer.Title>
            <Drawer.CloseTrigger />
          </Drawer.Header>
          <Drawer.Body>
              This drawer's state is controlled by the parent component.
              You can open/close it programmatically or through user interaction.
          </Drawer.Body>
          <Drawer.Footer>
            <Button variant="outline" onPress={() => setIsOpen(false)}>
              Cancel
            </Button>
            <Button onPress={() => setIsOpen(false)}>
              Save
            </Button>
          </Drawer.Footer>
        </Drawer.Content>
      </Drawer.Root>
    </Stack>
  );
}
```

overview.mdx:
## Overview

Drawers are overlay windows that appear on top of the main content to display
important information, gather user input, or require user decisions. They
temporarily disable the main interface until the user completes an action or
dismisses the drawer.

### Key features

- **Accessibility First**: Built with React Aria Components for WCAG 2.1 AA
  compliance
- **Flexible Positioning**: Support for left, right, top, and bottom placements
- **Responsive Sizing**: Multiple size variants from xs to full-screen
- **Smooth Animations**: Customizable motion presets for entrance/exit
- **Focus Management**: Automatic focus trapping and restoration
- **Keyboard Navigation**: Full keyboard support including Escape to close
- **Scroll Handling**: Options for inside or outside scrolling behavior

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[React Aria Drawer Docs](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Drawer.html)
[ARIA Drawer Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/)
[Figma Design Library](https://www.figma.com/design/gHbAJGfcrCv7f2bgzUQgHq/NIMBUS-Guidelines?node-id=1695-45519&m)

```jsx live
const App = () => {

    { value: "right", label: "Right (default)", description: "Slides in from the right side, commonly used for details panels and forms" },
    { value: "top", label: "Top", description: "Slides in from the top, useful for notifications or alerts" },
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Drawer.Root>
    <Drawer.Trigger>
      <Button>Open drawer</Button>
    </Drawer.Trigger>
    <Drawer.Content>
      <Drawer.Header>
        <Drawer.Title>Drawer Title</Drawer.Title>
        <Drawer.CloseTrigger />
      </Drawer.Header>
      <Drawer.Body>
          This is a basic drawer with default settings. It includes a backdrop overlay
          (enabled by default), title, and close button for a complete experience.
      </Drawer.Body>
      <Drawer.Footer>
        <Button variant="outline" slot="close">Cancel</Button>
        <Button>Save</Button>
      </Drawer.Footer>
    </Drawer.Content>
  </Drawer.Root>
)
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Role identification:** Drawer has `dialog` role with proper labeling.
- **Focus management:** Focus moves to drawer on open and returns to trigger on
  close.
- **Focus containment:** Tab navigation stays within drawer boundaries.
- **Accessible names:** Title and description properly associate with drawer.
- **State announcements:** Screen readers announce when drawer opens/closes.
- **Info and relationships:** Proper semantic structure with header, body, and
  footer.
- **Contrast:** All text meets minimum contrast requirements.
- **Non-text contrast:** Focus indicators and UI components meet contrast
  standards.
- **Keyboard navigation:** Full keyboard navigation support.
- **No keyboard trap:** Focus contained but escapable via Escape key.
- **Focus order:** Logical focus progression through drawer elements.
- **Focus visible:** Clear focus indicators on all interactive elements.
- **On focus:** No unexpected context changes when focusing elements.
- **On input:** Predictable behavior for all user interactions.
- **Labels or instructions:** Clear labeling and instructions where needed.
- **Name, role, value:** Proper semantic markup and ARIA attributes.
- **Status messages:** Appropriate announcements for state changes.

### Resources

- [W3C ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG) - Dialog](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/)
- [WCAG 2.1 Guidelines](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/quickref/)

dev.mdx:
## Getting started ### Import ```tsx import { Drawer } from '@commercetools/nimbus'; ``` ### Basic usage The Drawer component is a compound component with multiple parts that work together. This basic implementation shows a `Trigger` element that toggles the drawer's `Content` wrapper which contains `Header`, `Body`, and `Footer` sections: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Open Drawer Drawer Title This is the drawer body content. ) ``` ## Usage examples ### Placement options Control where the drawer appears on screen using the `placement` prop on `Drawer.Root`. Available placements are `left`, `right` (default), `top`, and `bottom`: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( {['left', 'right', 'top', 'bottom'].map((placement) => ( {placement} Placement: {placement} This drawer slides in from the {placement}. ))} ) ``` **Placement behavior:** - `placement="left"`: Slides in from the left edge, full viewport height - `placement="right"`: Slides in from the right edge, full viewport height (default) - `placement="top"`: Slides in from the top edge, full viewport width - `placement="bottom"`: Slides in from the bottom edge, full viewport width ### Backdrop variants Control whether to show a backdrop overlay behind the drawer using the `showBackdrop` prop (enabled by default): ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( No Backdrop No Backdrop This drawer has no backdrop overlay. With Backdrop With Backdrop This drawer has a blurred backdrop overlay. ) ``` **Backdrop behavior:** - `showBackdrop={true}`: Blurred backdrop overlay with semi-transparent background (default) - `showBackdrop={false}`: No backdrop overlay ### Custom trigger with asChild Use your own button or interactive element as the trigger using the `asChild` prop: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Custom Trigger This drawer was opened with a custom Button component. ) ``` ### Size control Control the drawer content width using style props on `Drawer.Content`. You can use design tokens or custom values: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( {['sm', 'md', 'lg', '512px', 'full'].map((size) => ( {size} Width: {size} This drawer uses width="{size}" on Drawer.Content. ))} ) ``` ### Scrollable content The drawer body automatically handles overflow with scroll support when content exceeds available height: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Open Scrollable Drawer Scrollable Content {Array.from({ length: 20 }, (_, i) => ( Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. This is paragraph {i + 1} of scrollable content. ))} ) ``` ### Dismissal control Control how users can dismiss the drawer using `isDismissable` and `isKeyboardDismissDisabled` props: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Fully Dismissable Fully Dismissable Can dismiss by: backdrop click, Escape key, or close button Modal Drawer Modal Drawer Can only dismiss using the close button ) ``` **Dismissal options:** - `isDismissable={true}`: Allow closing by clicking backdrop (default: true) - `isKeyboardDismissDisabled={false}`: Allow closing with Escape key (default: false) - Both can be combined for fine-grained control ### Intercepting close for unsaved changes Use `isDismissable={false}` with controlled mode to prevent accidental closing when forms have unsaved changes. Backdrop click is disabled, while Escape key and close buttons still fire `onOpenChange(false)`, allowing close attempts to be intercepted: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false); const [hasUnsavedChanges, setHasUnsavedChanges] = useState(true); const [showConfirmation, setShowConfirmation] = useState(false); const handleOpenChange = (open) => { if (open) { setIsOpen(true); return; } if (hasUnsavedChanges) { setShowConfirmation(true); return; } setIsOpen(false); }; return ( Edit Form Has unsaved changes {showConfirmation && ( You have unsaved changes. Discard them? )} ); } ``` **Key behavior:** - `isDismissable={false}`: Backdrop click is disabled. Escape and close buttons still fire `onOpenChange(false)` which can be intercepted to show a confirmation dialog. - Combine with controlled mode (`isOpen` + `onOpenChange`) to intercept close attempts - Toggle `isDismissable` dynamically based on form state (e.g. `isDismissable={!hasUnsavedChanges}`) ### Uncontrolled mode For simpler use cases, use uncontrolled mode with `defaultOpen` and `onOpenChange`: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const [lastAction, setLastAction] = useState('No action yet'); return ( { setLastAction(isOpen ? 'Drawer opened' : 'Drawer closed'); }} > Open Drawer Uncontrolled Drawer This drawer manages its own open state. Last action: {lastAction} ); } ``` Use uncontrolled mode when you only need to react to state changes without managing the state yourself. ### Controlled mode For scenarios requiring programmatic control or coordination with other components, use controlled mode: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false); return ( Drawer is {isOpen ? 'open' : 'closed'} Controlled Drawer This drawer's state is controlled externally via the Switch. ); } ``` Use controlled mode when you need to: - Synchronize the drawer state with external state - Open/close the drawer programmatically - Coordinate the drawer with other UI elements ## Component requirements ## Accessibility The Drawer component handles most accessibility requirements internally through React Aria Components. However, you must provide an accessible label for the drawer using one of these approaches: - **Using Drawer.Title** (recommended): ```tsx Drawer Title ... ``` - **Using aria-label** (when no visual title is needed): ```tsx Open ... ``` If your use case requires tracking and analytics for this component, it is good practice to add a **persistent**, **unique** id to the component: ```tsx const PERSISTENT_ID = "example-drawer"; export const Example = () => ( Open ... ); ``` #### Keyboard navigation The component supports full keyboard interaction: - `Tab` / `Shift+Tab`: Navigate between focusable elements inside the drawer - `Escape`: Close the drawer (unless `isKeyboardDismissDisabled` is true) - Focus is automatically trapped within the drawer when open - Focus is restored to the trigger element when the drawer closes #### ARIA attributes - `role="dialog"`: Applied to the drawer content - `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`: Associates the drawer with its title - `aria-modal="true"`: Indicates the drawer is modal (blocks interaction with background) ## API reference ## Common patterns ### Nested drawers Drawers can be nested to create multi-level workflows. Each drawer maintains proper z-index stacking: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Open First Drawer First Level This is the first level drawer. Second Level This nested drawer appears above the first with proper z-index. ) ``` ### Conditional dismissal with shouldCloseOnInteractOutside Control whether the drawer should close when clicking outside based on custom logic: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const [hasUnsavedChanges, setHasUnsavedChanges] = useState(false); return ( { if (hasUnsavedChanges) { alert('You have unsaved changes!'); return false; } return true; }} > Open Editor Document Editor Make changes to simulate unsaved data: Has unsaved changes Try clicking outside when changes are unsaved ); } ``` ## Testing your implementation These examples demonstrate how to test your implementation when using Drawer in your application. As the component's internal functionality is already tested by Nimbus, these patterns help you verify your integration and application-specific logic. ### Basic Rendering Tests Verify the drawer renders with expected elements ```tsx import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { useState } from "react"; import { Drawer, Button, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Drawer - Basic rendering", () => { it("renders trigger button", () => { render( Open Drawer Content ); expect( screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Open Drawer" }) ).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("does not render dialog when closed", () => { render( Open Content ); expect(screen.queryByRole("dialog")).not.toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ### Interaction Tests Test drawer opening and closing behavior ```tsx import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { useState } from "react"; import { Drawer, Button, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Drawer - Interactions", () => { it("opens drawer when trigger is clicked", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( Open Drawer Test Drawer Content ); const trigger = screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Open Drawer" }); await user.click(trigger); await waitFor(() => { expect(screen.getByRole("dialog")).toBeInTheDocument(); expect( screen.getByRole("heading", { name: "Test Drawer" }) ).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); it("closes drawer when close button is clicked", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( Open Test Content ); await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Open" })); await waitFor(() => { expect(screen.getByRole("dialog")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); const closeButton = screen.getByRole("button", { name: /close/i }); await user.click(closeButton); await waitFor(() => { expect(screen.queryByRole("dialog")).not.toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); it("closes drawer when Escape key is pressed", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( Open Content ); await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Open" })); await waitFor(() => { expect(screen.getByRole("dialog")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); await user.keyboard("{Escape}"); await waitFor(() => { expect(screen.queryByRole("dialog")).not.toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); }); ``` ### Controlled State Tests Test controlled mode behavior ```tsx import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { useState } from "react"; import { Drawer, Button, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Drawer - Controlled state", () => { it("opens and closes via controlled prop", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); const ControlledDrawer = () => { const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false); return ( Content ); }; render(); expect(screen.queryByRole("dialog")).not.toBeInTheDocument(); await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "External Open" })); await waitFor(() => { expect(screen.getByRole("dialog")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); it("calls onOpenChange when drawer state changes", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); const handleOpenChange = vi.fn(); render( Open Content ); await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Open" })); await waitFor(() => { expect(handleOpenChange).toHaveBeenCalledWith(true); }); await user.keyboard("{Escape}"); await waitFor(() => { expect(handleOpenChange).toHaveBeenCalledWith(false); }); }); }); ``` ### Portal Content Tests Test drawer content rendering in portal ```tsx import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { useState } from "react"; import { Drawer, Button, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Drawer - Portal content", () => { it("renders drawer content in portal", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( Open
Portal Content
 ); await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Open" })); await waitFor(() => { const dialog = screen.getByRole("dialog"); expect(dialog).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByTestId("portal-content")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); }); ``` ### Dismissal Behavior Tests Test different dismissal configurations ```tsx import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { useState } from "react"; import { Drawer, Button, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Drawer - Dismissal", () => { it("prevents Escape key dismissal when isKeyboardDismissDisabled", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( Open Content ); await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Open" })); await waitFor(() => { expect(screen.getByRole("dialog")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); await user.keyboard("{Escape}"); // Drawer should still be open expect(screen.getByRole("dialog")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("accepts shouldCloseOnInteractOutside callback prop", () => { const shouldClose = vi.fn(() => false); render( Open Content ); // Verify component renders with the callback prop without errors expect(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Open" })).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ## Resources - [Storybook](https://nimbus-storybook.vercel.app/?path=/docs/components-overlay-drawer--docs) - [React Aria DialogTrigger](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Dialog.html#dialogtrigger) - [React Aria Modal](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Modal.html) - [ARIA Dialog Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/)

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Use drawers strategically to enhance user workflow without disrupting the
experience.

### Best practices

- **Use drawers sparingly**: Only when you need to interrupt the user's flow for
  critical actions
- **Keep content focused**: Drawers should have a single, clear purpose
- **Provide clear actions**: Always include obvious ways to proceed or dismiss
- **Make dismissal easy**: Support Escape key, close buttons, and click-outside
  behavior. For forms with unsaved changes, use `isDismissable={false}` with
  controlled mode to intercept close attempts and show a confirmation before
  discarding data
- **Maintain focus flow**: Focus should move logically through interactive
  elements
- **Size appropriately**: Choose sizes that fit your content without
  overwhelming the viewport. For complex full-page editing workflows, use the
  ModalPage component instead
- **Consider mobile**: Ensure drawers work well on small screens

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Confirming destructive actions**: Delete confirmations and critical warnings
- **Collecting focused input**: Forms, settings, and data entry that requires
  attention
- **Displaying critical alerts**: Important information that requires immediate
  user attention
- **Showing detailed information**: Content that doesn't require navigation to a
  new page
- **Authentication flows**: Login, signup, and security-related interactions

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Complex multi-step workflows**: Use pages instead for lengthy processes
- **Non-critical information**: Use inline content for supplementary information
- **Navigation**: Use proper routing instead of drawers for moving between
  sections
- **Content that needs to be referenced**: Avoid for information users need
  while working elsewhere
